# Changelog

**Spoolman NG** is a community-maintained continuation of [Spoolman](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman) by Donkie. Full per-release notes (auto-generated from merged pull requests) are published on the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/sherrmann/Spoolman-NG/releases) page; this file summarizes notable changes.

## Unreleased

- **New: Scan-to-Spool — photograph a label, get a prefilled create form** (#361) — the scan dialog gains a **Photo** tab (phones open the camera directly; photos are downscaled in-browser before upload). The configured vision model reads the label into structured fields (vendor, name, material, weight, diameter, temperatures, lot number, article number — never guessing what it can't see), and Spoolman matches the result **against your own filament library first** (a known filament just gains a spool — no duplicate records), then the locally-synced [SpoolmanDB](https://github.com/Donkie/SpoolmanDB) catalog, with the raw extraction as an always-available fallback. The review step hands off to the normal create forms, prefilled and fully editable — nothing is created until you save. Photos are ephemeral by design: held in memory for the request, never written to disk, database, or logs. Extraction and matching are separate endpoints (`POST /api/v1/ai/spool-intake/extract`, `POST /api/v1/ai/spool-intake/match`) joined by a stable extraction-JSON contract, so a future companion app running an on-device vision model can reuse the same matching without any photo leaving the phone. Both endpoints answer 404 until the feature toggle is on.
- **New: AI provider foundation** (#359) — the shared plumbing every AI feature builds on, inert until configured: a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint config (env vars `SPOOLMAN_AI_BASE_URL`/`_API_KEY`/`_MODEL`/`_VISION_MODEL` are authoritative over the new **Settings → AI** tab; env-set fields show locked in the UI), provider presets as data (Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Requesty, Groq, Mistral, Gemini), and a capability probe (`GET /api/v1/ai/status`, `POST /api/v1/ai/probe`) reporting reachability, the endpoint's model list, and honest per-capability verdicts — Ollama endpoints are enriched via Ollama's own API; generic endpoints report *not verified* rather than a guess. Feature toggles (all **off** by default) grey out with the reason inline when their requirements aren't met — e.g. Scan-to-Spool with a model that has no vision support. The API key is **write-only**: stored outside the settings registry (which echoes and broadcasts registered values), never returned by any endpoint, cleared or replaced but never shown. A stock install renders zero new UI outside the settings tab and sends nothing anywhere.
- **New: Home Assistant ingress support** (#211) — the server can now render the web UI for HA's embedded sidebar panel. With `SPOOLMAN_HA_INGRESS=1` (set only by the [HA add-on](https://github.com/sherrmann/spoolman-ng-addons)'s run script) the three path-dependent responses — index.html's asset paths, the PWA manifest's `start_url`/`scope`, and `/config.js` — are rendered per-request for the rotating per-session prefix HA passes in the `X-Ingress-Path` header (strictly validated before it is ever reflected; anything malformed falls back to the configured base path). Under ingress the client skips service-worker registration, since no SW scope can follow a rotating token path. Strictly additive: requests without the header — the direct host port, with the full PWA, QR deep links and API/websocket integrations — serve byte-identical bodies to before, and root/`SPOOLMAN_BASE_PATH` deployments outside HA are unchanged (the header is ignored entirely without the env flag). index.html, the manifest and `/config.js` are now served with `Cache-Control: no-store` in all modes so no cache can pin a dead session path. Covered end-to-end by a simulated ingress gateway in front of the real backend: boot, API, live-update websocket and token rotation inside the prefix, plus unchanged direct access on the same process.
- **New: per-install-type update action** (#294) — the "update available" hint (#293) is now clickable and opens a dialog tailored to how Spoolman was installed. **Native** installs get a real **Update now** button that runs the bundled `scripts/update.sh` in the background (under systemd the service restarts itself; otherwise the dialog says to restart manually). **Docker** installs get the `docker compose pull && up -d` steps plus an image-tag reminder; **Home Assistant** add-ons are pointed at Supervisor's own update UI. Triggering the updater from a browser is remote code execution by design, so the endpoint is **admin-only** whenever authentication is configured and, on an open (no-auth) instance, stays disabled unless `SPOOLMAN_ALLOW_UI_UPDATE=TRUE` is set explicitly; only the bundled script runs, and the sole caller input is an optional `--tag` validated against `^v[0-9.]+$`. `GET /api/v1/info` additively reports `install_type` and `update_action_available`.
- **New: from-scratch order builder** (#324) — the Orders page's **"+ New order"** button was a disabled placeholder; orders could only be born from the Low Stock flows (mark-as-ordered / bulk create). It now opens a builder that adds a filament picker + lines editor on top of the order details modal's header surface (shop, order date, order number, link, comment): pick any filaments, set each line's quantity and price, add/remove lines, and create the order in one step. Create is gated until at least one line has a filament chosen.
- **Removed: per-spool colour override** (revert of #74) — colour is an intrinsic property of a filament *product* (a red and a blue Sunlu PLA are different products), so it is modelled only on the filament. The per-spool override added a second, conflicting source of truth for the same attribute and forced every swatch surface — and every external API consumer — to know a `spool ?? filament` fallback rule. The three `spool` colour columns (`color_hex`, `multi_color_hexes`, `multi_color_direction`), the spool create/edit colour picker, and the API fields are gone; a migration drops the columns (any override a spool had stored is discarded — its swatch simply shows the filament colour, exactly as an unset override already did). The "different colour of the same filament" workflow is served by the filament **Clone** action instead. Integrations (Moonraker/OctoPrint/Home Assistant, NFC tags, the colour-similarity filter) were unaffected either way, since they always read the filament's colour.
- **New: deployment-catalog templates (Zeabur, Unraid, BigBear) + NAS-catalog docs** (#266) — submission-ready packaging for the Docker image now lives in-repo: `integrations/zeabur/` (one-click Zeabur template, SQLite default plus a PostgreSQL variant — each deployable into your own account with `npx zeabur@latest template deploy -f template.yaml`), `integrations/unraid/` (Unraid Docker-manager XML template with Unraid-appropriate defaults — appdata path, `PUID`/`PGID` 99/100 — usable today by dropping it into `templates-user/`, ready for a Community Apps listing), and `integrations/bigbear/` (compose entry in BigBear/CasaOS catalog conventions). The installation guide gains a one-click-catalogs section plus a TrueCharts/TrueNAS SCALE note: override the image repository to `ghcr.io/sherrmann/spoolman-ng` and keep everything else — port 8000, the data volume, `SPOOLMAN_*` env vars, the `/api/v1/health` probe — unchanged. The external catalog submissions are tracked per target: #335 (Zeabur marketplace), #336 (Unraid Community Apps), #337 (BigBear).
- **New: KIAUH v6 community extension** (#270) — KIAUH v5 had a built-in (upstream) Spoolman installer; v6 moved to community-maintained extensions, and `integrations/kiauh/` now ships one for Spoolman NG. From KIAUH's Extensions menu it installs the latest release zip natively into `~/Spoolman` (port prompt included), wires Moonraker — the `[update_manager Spoolman]` *type: zip* recipe (#263), the `[spoolman]` filament-tracking section, and the `moonraker.asvc` entry — and offers in-place update and clean remove (database preserved by default). One-liner to add it to a KIAUH clone: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sherrmann/Spoolman-NG/master/integrations/kiauh/install-extension.sh | bash`.
- **New: filament reference photos** (#88) — attach a photo to a filament (e.g. of the spool or the shop listing) so visually unusual filaments — glitter, transparent PETG — are easy to recognize. Uploaded via `PUT /filament/{id}/image` (raw JPEG/PNG/WebP body, 2 MB cap, no multipart) and served with ETag/304 caching from `GET /filament/{id}/image`; the filament payload gains a `has_image` flag. The browser downscales photos to ≤1024 px before upload and strips metadata such as GPS coordinates; bytes are stored as database blobs, so every existing backup path (SQLite rotation, external-DB dumps) captures them unchanged. The photo shows on the filament page and replaces the colour tile in the gallery view, with upload on the create and edit forms.
- **Reverted the unreleased filament 'ordered' fields** (#298/#309) — the three flat `ordered_at`/`order_url`/`order_note` columns that landed unreleased in #309 modelled order state as a property of a product type (couldn't group a bulk order, capture arrival into spools, or hold a shop). They are removed before any release wired them into the stable v1 API, and superseded by first-class **Orders & Shops** (Phase 1, below).
- **New: Shops** (#298) — a first-class `/shop` entity (name, homepage, region ships-to list, comment), distinct from the manufacturer Vendor, for tracking where filament is reordered.
- **New: Orders** (#298) — a `/order` entity grouping the lines of one bulk reorder (per-line filament, quantity, price). Order state (open/arrived) is derived from its lines; a PATCH with `lines` fully replaces the line set. Deleting an order cascades its lines; a shop with orders, or a filament with order lines, cannot be deleted — both reject the delete with **409 Conflict** and a message naming the references (#320), so clients key on one status for the referenced-cannot-delete case.
- **New: order arrival** (#298) — `POST /order/{id}/arrive` marks lines arrived and optionally turns the delivered quantities into spools (carrying each line's price and an optional location). Partial deliveries split a line into an arrived part and an outstanding remainder, so split shipments need no extra order states.
- **Fixed: order arrival is now one transaction** (#322) — arrival previously committed the line mutations first and then created each spool in its own commit, so a failure part-way through spool creation left lines marked arrived with only some of their spools and no way to retry (re-arrive rejects arrived lines). Line arrival and spool creation now commit together: any failure rolls the whole arrival back, so the order stays fully outstanding and can be arrived cleanly on retry.
- **New: filament `on_order` field** (#298) — the filament list and detail endpoints now expose `on_order` = `{order_id, ordered_at}` of the oldest open order containing the filament (null when nothing is outstanding). Home Assistant / HACS automations can read it to quiet low-stock alerts once a replenishment is on its way.
- **Fixed: label preview now sits beside the settings** (#296) — a global layout rule (from the dashboard redesign) silently forced the print dialog's two columns to stack at every window size, leaving half the screen empty. Wide screens get preview + settings side by side; below ~1000 px they stack intentionally.
- **New: pre-print checklist** (#296) — clicking Print first shows the geometry checks that browser print dialogs commonly break: scale 100 % (never "fit to page"), the exact paper size in mm, margins off, headers/footers off. When a label paper size is used with automatic page sizing, a one-click "Match label size" fix is offered. Dismissible with "don't show again".
- **New: label QR size setting** (#295) — the printed QR square is now adjustable in mm from the label dialogs (Auto keeps the old fill-half-the-label behavior); a soft warning flags sizes under 10 mm as hard to phone-scan. Also fixes the QR overflowing its half of narrow labels: the SVG's intrinsic size no longer overrides the layout split.
- **Deployment-arc hardening**: upgrades now carry *data* in the tests (native update and `docker compose pull`-style image swap both prove pre-upgrade spools survive migrations); a new CI `verify-artifact` job runs the release-zip contract against the candidate artifact *before* anything publishes; the native install matrix gains an **arm64 (Pi-class) leg** under QEMU; third-party consumer checkouts auto-refresh weekly and suites log consumer versions; suites refresh the local `:latest` image once per session (stale caches silently tested old releases).
- **Image publishing polish** (#273) — multi-arch images now carry BuildKit **SBOM and provenance attestations**, and releases sync a proper overview to the Docker Hub page (`cookiemonster95/spoolman-ng`), which was previously blank.
- **Docs: RPi Imager integrations & compatibility matrix** (#315) — the README Integrations section now carries a table for the Raspberry Pi Imager **3D printing** catalog (OctoPi, OctoKlipperPi, Mainsail OS, PrintWatch OS, SimplyPrint, DuetPi, Repetier-Server, 3DPrinterOS), stating whether and how each appliance works with Spoolman, plus adjacent tools (Fluidd, KlipperScreen, Home Assistant, OctoEverywhere). Cross-links the sibling integration issues (#312–#314).
- **Docs: reverse proxies & networking** (#275) — canonical recipes in the installation guide: Caddy, nginx (with the WebSocket upgrade headers live updates need), Traefik incl. sub-path via `SPOOLMAN_BASE_PATH`, auth-at-proxy caveats for printer traffic, IPv6 (`SPOOLMAN_HOST=::`), and a rootless-Podman quadlet unit.
- **New: `scripts/update.sh`** (#271) — one-command in-place updates for native installs without Moonraker: overlays the new release (preserving `.env`, `.venv`, `uv/`), re-syncs dependencies (keeping the NFC extra when present), seeds pip, and restarts the systemd service. Covered by an e2e that upgrades a real previous-release install.
- **New: official Helm chart** (#274) — published with every release as an OCI artifact: `helm install spoolman oci://ghcr.io/sherrmann/charts/spoolman-ng`. Single replica by design (SQLite is single-writer), PVC-backed data directory, health probes, non-root security context, `SPOOLMAN_*` env passthrough, optional secret-file database password, and ingress with `SPOOLMAN_BASE_PATH` support. Verified by a new k3d install test (`tests_deployment/run.sh helm`); chart lint runs in CI.
- **Fixed: Moonraker one-click updates were broken four ways** (#261–#264, found by the deployment-targets audit): `release_info.json` now names the GitHub repo exactly (`Spoolman-NG`) so Moonraker's update checks work — existing native installs need the one-time fix in the [migration note](docs/installation.md#one-click-updates-from-moonraker-klipper-users); release titles are now the bare tag so Moonraker stops showing a perpetual "update available"; the release zip ships a root `requirements.txt` and the installer seeds `pip` into `.venv`, enabling the newly documented `type: zip` updater recipe (dependency reinstalls + service restart) — the previous `type: web` recipe, which **wiped `.venv` and `.env` on first update**, is gone from the docs; and `scripts/*.sh` inside the zip are executable again, so the documented install one-liner works.
- **New: deployment-channel contract tests (Tier 1)** in `tests_deployment/` (#277) — a locally runnable harness (`tests_deployment/run.sh`) that exercises the published artifacts through their real consumers: the release-zip contract, `scripts/install.sh` on Debian/Fedora/Arch containers, **real Moonraker** validating the updater recipe via its own API, and the Home Assistant add-on options contract. Red assertions cite the issue they pin; `SPOOLMAN_ZIP_PATH` lets them verify unreleased artifacts.

## [2026.7.10] — 2026-07-11

- **Digital Asset Links: `handle_all_urls` served too** — Bitwarden (and other credential managers that validate app↔site association themselves) require the `handle_all_urls` relation in `/.well-known/assetlinks.json`; the server now serves both relations, so in-app passkey flows work with third-party credential managers as well.

## [2026.7.9] — 2026-07-11

- **Frictionless passkey setup**: the server now serves `/.well-known/assetlinks.json` itself (no manual reverse-proxy config), and the companion app gained an in-app **passkey setup assistant** that reads the app identity natively and walks through the domain association.
- **Companion app passkeys**: the login WebView supports WebAuthn ceremonies (via the WebView's `WEB_AUTHENTICATION_SUPPORT_FOR_APP` mode), so passkey login through a forward-auth portal (e.g. Authelia) works inside the app.
- **Companion app self-update (Android)**: the app checks GitHub releases and can download and install the release APK in-app.

## [2026.7.8] — 2026-07-10

- **Companion app: forward-auth gateways supported** — a server behind Authelia or a similar portal is detected during setup, and login completes through an in-WebView portal flow instead of a dead-end connection error.

## [2026.7.7] — 2026-07-10

- **Fixed: auth blocked every intended-open endpoint on real deployments.** With an API token or user accounts configured, `/api/v1/health`, `/docs`, `/redoc`, `/auth/status` and even `/auth/login` returned 401 — making password login impossible and breaking health monitoring. The middleware compared its sub-app-relative open-path list against the full request path (Starlette keeps the mount prefix in `root_path`); the unit harness drove the app unmounted, which is why tests stayed green. Found by the Home Assistant add-on live-testing round; now covered by mounted-app regression tests including `SPOOLMAN_BASE_PATH` prefixes.
- **Home Assistant add-on moved to a dedicated repository**: [`sherrmann/spoolman-ng-addons`](https://github.com/sherrmann/spoolman-ng-addons) — Supervisor requires the repository manifest at the git root, so the in-tree `ha-addon/` packaging could only ever be installed by manual copy. The add-on is now installable by URL from the HA add-on store, and every release pushes a matching version bump there so updates appear in the HA UI automatically.
- **API docs deploy fixed**: docs now deploy from master pushes — tag refs are blocked by the Pages environment.

## [2026.7.6] — 2026-07-10

- **Translations completed for every language**: the ~300 keys per locale that accumulated untranslated over the 2026.7.x feature wave (auth, printers, locations, statistics, weigh workflow, bulk edit, …) are now translated in all locales, with correct language-specific plural forms. CI now enforces **placeholder integrity** — a translation that drops a `{{variable}}` or `<tag>` fails the build (this immediately caught 34 stale/broken values, now fixed).
- **UK English added and made the default**: `en-GB` (British spellings, UK date formats) is used unless the browser or the user picks another language; the previous `en` remains available as *English (US)* — and its date pickers now consistently use US formats instead of the old US-strings/UK-formats mix.
- **Home dashboard**: the redundant page title/subtitle is gone; the "Value" KPI is now an honest **estimated stock value** (spool price falling back to filament price, scaled by remaining weight — previously it summed only explicit spool prices); the filament KPI footer shows the distinct material count instead of the meaningless "all synced".
- **Spool list**: the totals row is always visible and sums the **selected** spools when a selection exists (otherwise the shown page); *Show Archived* now shows **only** archived spools (new `archived` query filter on `GET /api/v1/spool`); the bulk bar offers Archive or Unarchive to match the active view; the filament-name link uses the normal text colour; toolbar buttons have tooltips; *Hide Columns* is now *Columns*.
- **Colour filter**: leads with a one-click palette of common filament colours (full picker behind *More colors*), and the toolbar button shows the active colour.
- **Spool page**: a summary card with swatch, tags, stock bar and key numbers; a *Sibling spools* header button that lists every spool of the same filament; adjusting filament now refreshes the usage history immediately.
- **Filament list**: aligned with the spool list — inline editing (material, price, article number, comment), a colour-tile grid view, the Columns manager with drag-to-reorder/resize, and toolbar tooltips.
- **Label printing**: the live preview warns when label content is clipped or the grid cannot fit the paper.
- **Home Assistant add-on**: fixed the base image name (`spoolman-ng`) so the add-on actually builds; `poe bump` now keeps the add-on version in step; documented the working local-add-on install path.
- **Release pipeline**: images/releases now require the unit, client and e2e suites to pass; API docs redeploy on release tags.

## [2026.7.0] – [2026.7.5] — 2026-07

Six releases; full auto-generated notes on the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/sherrmann/Spoolman-NG/releases) page. Highlights:

- **Repository renamed to [`sherrmann/Spoolman-NG`](https://github.com/sherrmann/Spoolman-NG).** Old `github.com/sherrmann/Spoolman` links — including existing Moonraker `repo:` entries — keep working through GitHub's redirects. The API docs moved to <https://sherrmann.github.io/Spoolman-NG/> with **no** redirect from the old address.
- **Docker images renamed** to `ghcr.io/sherrmann/spoolman-ng` and `cookiemonster95/spoolman-ng`. The old `ghcr.io/sherrmann/spoolman` / `cookiemonster95/spoolman` images stay pullable but are frozen at the last tag published before the rename — update the `image:` line in your compose file; data volumes and settings are unaffected.
- **Optional user accounts** (password login, admin/read-only roles), API tokens, and an experimental Home Assistant add-on package.
- **Locations** as a first-class entity with custom fields, scannable location labels and scan-to-move; **printer** entity with spool assignment.
- **List upgrades**: free-text search, colour-similarity filter, bulk edit/archive, weigh-spools workflow, drag-to-reorder/resize columns, hue sorting, totals row, grid view, inline editing, unit auto-scaling.
- **Spool intelligence**: usage & cost statistics (`/api/v1/stats/usage`), per-spool usage-event history and weight-history chart, per-spool colour/diameter overrides, idempotent use/measure endpoints.
- **Filament & import**: slicer-profile export, sliced-`.3mf` bulk adjust, retail-barcode lookup, SpoolmanDB catalog descriptors, temperature ranges.
- The client bundle is now built inside the Dockerfile, so `docker build .` works from a clean checkout.

## [2026.6.0] — 2026-06-30

First release of the Spoolman NG fork, built on upstream Spoolman 0.23.1.

- **NFC spool identification** — TigerTag, OpenPrintTag, and QIDI tags. Included in the Docker images for all architectures (`amd64`, `arm64`, `armv7`).
- **Filament label printing** with separate presets, QR codes, and filament QR scanning support.
- **Redesigned home dashboard** with KPI cards and inventory analytics.
- Merged upstream community PRs: extra-field filter/sort, 3D Filament Profiles import, weight-delta events, and calibration.
- **Fork infrastructure**: multi-arch images published to GHCR (`ghcr.io/sherrmann/spoolman`) and Docker Hub (`cookiemonster95/spoolman`); CalVer releases with `:latest`, `:edge`, and `:sha-*` tags; and one-click updates via Moonraker's `update_manager`.
